name: I slipped and fell at work in Murray but didn't tell my boss right away, what should I do now?

mainEntity.name: I slipped and fell at work in Murray but didn't tell my boss right away, what should I do now?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report to employer ASAP (Utah allows 180 days), seek medical care, file claim—delays hurt but don't end your case.
acceptedAnswer.description: Report the injury to your employer immediately, even if delayed, as Utah law requires notice within 180 days of the injury or when you knew it was work-related. Delays can weaken your claim but don't automatically bar it if you have good reason. Key factors: Prompt medical care documents the injury; witnesses or incident reports help prove it happened at work; Utah's workers' comp is no-fault so your delay matters less than proof of connection. Get treated by approved doctors, file Form 12 with the Utah Labor Commission, and consult a workers' comp attorney to avoid denial. Acting fast preserves benefits like medical costs and lost wages.

description: Report to employer ASAP (Utah allows 180 days), seek medical care, file claim—delays hurt but don't end your case.
